channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Did Viking 1 find life on Mars 50 years ago? Here's why the debate continues - Space
item.description:
1. Did Viking 1 find life on Mars 50 years ago? Here's why the debate continues  Space
  2. From Hampton to Mars: How NASA Langley Helped Land on the Red Planet  NASA (.gov)
  3. Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars  The Atlantic
  4. On This Day: Viking 1 is first spacecraft to land on Mars  upi.com
  5. Monday briefing: 50 years after Viking landed on Mars, did Nasa overlook evidence of life?  The Guardian

item.title: Mysterious 'little red dots' at the beginning time may finally have an explanation, thanks to newfound 'little blue companions' - Live Science
item.description:
1. Mysterious 'little red dots' at the beginning time may finally have an explanation, thanks to newfound 'little blue companions'  Live Science
  2. One idea, two cosmic mysteries—linking Little Red Dots and globular clusters  Phys.org
  3. Little red dot galaxies may solve a cosmic neutrino mystery  The Brighter Side of News
  4. A mysterious “red dot” spotted in space: are we witnessing a cosmic birth?  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  5. The James Webb Space Telescope found tiny “little red dots” that looked like impossibly compact galaxies — but astronomers now suspect many may be “black hole stars,” young black holes wrapped in gas so dense that the material falling into them produces  Space Daily

item.title: NASA has just released a jaw-dropping video of a spacecraft's view as it zoomed past Mars. You've got to see this - BBC Sky at Night Magazine
item.description:
1. NASA has just released a jaw-dropping video of a spacecraft's view as it zoomed past Mars. You've got to see this  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  2. NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. NASA Shares Beautiful Timelapse Of The Psyche Spacecraft's View Over Mars  Engadget
  4. NASA’s Psyche Spacecraft Captures Mind-Bending Month-Long Timelapse of Mars  PetaPixel
  5. NASA's Psyche mission delivers Mars flyby data, time-lapse video  Phys.org

item.title: Earth’s Oceans Are Rapidly Losing Oxygen. It Could Destabilize the Planet - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. Earth’s Oceans Are Rapidly Losing Oxygen. It Could Destabilize the Planet  Gizmodo
  2. Earth’s waters are quietly running out of oxygen, scientists warn  ScienceDaily
  3. Oxygen "disappearing" from earth's waters at alarming rate, scientists warn  Oceanographic Magazine
  4. Underwater oxygen loss threatens earth's stability, researchers warn  Phys.org
  5. Rapid ocean oxygen loss pushing Earth to ‘unsafe space’  newsreel.com.au

item.title: Earth-like LHS 1140b May Feature The First Atmosphere Found On Exoplanet - Hackaday
item.description:
1. Earth-like LHS 1140b May Feature The First Atmosphere Found On Exoplanet  Hackaday
  2. First atmosphere found on Earth-like planet in habitable zone of distant star  BBC
  3. Harvard scientists detect atmosphere on distant ‘Earth-like’ planet  Harvard University
  4. Astronomers discover 1st atmosphere around a rocky Earth-like planet in the habitable zone  Space
  5. Earth-like exoplanet found to have an atmosphere  The Guardian

item.title: Benin's presumed-dead coral reef rediscovered teeming with life - Oceanographic Magazine
item.description:
1. Benin's presumed-dead coral reef rediscovered teeming with life  Oceanographic Magazine
  2. After More Than 60 Years Presumed-Dead A Coral Reef Is Alive and Thriving Off The Coast Of Benin  IFLScience
  3. Underwater drones discover coral reefs off Benin, long presumed dead, teeming with life  Phys.org
  4. The Thriving Coral Reef That ‘Defeated Time’ in West Africa  Inside Climate News
  5. “It Was a Mix of Excitement and Disbelief”: When Scientists Sent Deep-Sea Rovers to Confirm a 1960s Theory, They Made a Surprising Discovery  The Debrief

item.title: An artificial 'Saturn's ring' of satellites could soon fill the sky, astronomers warn - Space
item.description:
1. An artificial 'Saturn's ring' of satellites could soon fill the sky, astronomers warn  Space
  2. There are already 16,000 satellites in Earth’s orbit. How will we manage the next 100,000?  The Conversation
  3. Vantablack: World's Blackest Paint Could Solve A Major Problem For Astronomy  Yahoo
  4. Millions of satellites poised to enter space pose ‘grave threat’ to night skies  Geographical
  5. 'Catastrophic': Million-satellite 'ring' around Earth could be crippling for observational astronomy  starlust.org

item.title: Meteorite that crashed into NJ home holds building blocks of life, scientists say - Fox News
item.description:
1. Meteorite that crashed into NJ home holds building blocks of life, scientists say  Fox News

  3. Meteorite that crashed into New Jersey home contains "alien world chemistry," scientists say  CBS News
  4. Meteorite that crashed into a New Jersey home contains ‘extraterrestrial’ amino acids  CNN
  5. NASA finds clues to early solar system and life’s origins in pristine 2024 meteorite discovered in New Jersey  FOX Weather

item.description:
1. The Perseid meteor shower is here and skywatchers could see up to 100 shooting stars per hour  FOX Weather
  2. Year's Most Popular Meteor Shower Begins: Ideal Peak Viewing Ahead  DailyVoice.com
  3. How and when to see the 2026 Perseid meteor shower  BBC
  4. The Perseid meteor shower starts this week. Here's why you should look up now  Space
  5. State Parks Offering Free Viewing Hours For Highly Anticipated Perseids Meteor Showers  Patch

item.title: New Metal Alloy Is up to 10 Times Stronger Than Structural Steel - SciTechDaily
item.description: New Metal Alloy Is up to 10 Times Stronger Than Structural Steel  SciTechDaily

item.title: In 1977, NASA launched two probes designed to last five years. Voyager 1 and 2 are now the only human-made objects in interstellar space, still operating, still transmitting, funded by a team of engineers some of whom have worked on the mission for nearly 5 - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In 1977, NASA launched two probes designed to last five years. Voyager 1 and 2 are now the only human-made objects in interstellar space, still operating, still transmitting, funded by a team of engineers some of whom have worked on the mission for nearly 5  Space Daily
  2. Voyager 1 on track to reach one light-day from Earth  it-online.co.za
  3. NASA's Voyager 1 Radio Transmitter Is Weaker Than a Refrigerator Lightbulb. It Still Reaches Earth From 15 Billion Miles Away  19FortyFive
  4. Voyager 1 is still transmitting from more than 25 billion kilometres away, powered by less than 250 watts of decaying plutonium — a signal that now takes over 23 hours to reach Earth, carried by a radio no stronger than a refrigerator lightbulb  Make Tech Easier
  5. The engineers who built Voyager knew they would almost certainly be dead before it left the solar system, and they built it anyway, a gift from people who would never see where their work would go  ScienceBlog.com

item.title: We’re Underestimating the Power of ‘One-in-a-Thousand-Year’ Solar Storms, NASA Warns - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. We’re Underestimating the Power of ‘One-in-a-Thousand-Year’ Solar Storms, NASA Warns  Gizmodo
  2. New NASA Study Says Possibly No Limit to Solar Storm Effects  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Regression to the mean can explain saturation of geomagnetic storms  Nature
  4. Solar storms could be even worse than we thought, scientists warn  The Independent
  5. Are we underestimating the threat of solar storms? A 'once-in-a-thousand-year' disaster is worth considering, scientists say  Space

item.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves - Space
item.description:
1. James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves  Space
  2. NASA’s James Webb catches a supermassive black hole feeding  ScienceDaily
  3. NASA's James Webb Telescope Maps How Supermassive Black Holes Gain Mass  NDTV
  4. JWST captures black hole recycling its own fuel  MSN
  5. The James Webb Space Telescope spent nearly eight hours staring into NGC 4696, a galaxy 145 million light-years away, and watched a river of cool gas pour into an 800-light-year disk spinning at 600 kilometers per second, feeding the supermassive black  Space Daily

item.title: A radio structure thought for 40 years to tower over the Milky Way’s centre is actually a nearby stellar bubble — not the fossil scar of a black hole eruption - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A radio structure thought for 40 years to tower over the Milky Way’s centre is actually a nearby stellar bubble — not the fossil scar of a black hole eruption  Space Daily
  2. A Giant Structure Is Towering Over The Milky Way. After 40 Years, Scientists Have Worked Out What It Is  ScienceAlert
  3. Scientists solve 40-year mystery of the "lobe" above the center of the Milky Way  NEWS.am TECH

item.title: Astronauts returning from long stays in orbit say the smell of rain is almost too much to bear and a familiar street feels like something they are piloting rather than driving - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Astronauts returning from long stays in orbit say the smell of rain is almost too much to bear and a familiar street feels like something they are piloting rather than driving  Space Daily

  3. Astronauts insist space has a smell — a mix of seared steak, hot metal and welding fumes that clings to a suit after every spacewalk  Space Daily

  4. Mines researcher helps put “silly sprinklers” in reverse to unravel decades-old physics puzzle  Mines Newsroom

item.title: In May 1973, Skylab reached orbit with its heat shield torn away, one solar wing gone and the other jammed, leaving America’s first space station at roughly 54°C — so engineers built a parasol in days and astronauts carried it up to save the laboratory - Space Daily
item.description: In May 1973, Skylab reached orbit with its heat shield torn away, one solar wing gone and the other jammed, leaving America’s first space station at roughly 54°C — so engineers built a parasol in days and astronauts carried it up to save the laboratory  Space Daily

  4. Bristle Worm Jaws Incorporate Metal Ions to Mimic Synthetic Alloys  geneonline.com

  3. Sun holds 55% more silver than estimated, new model reveals  Phys.org
  4. Astronomers were wrong about the amount of silver contained in the Sun  Universe Space Tech
  5. Study finds the Sun contains 55% more silver than previously estimated  Milliyet

item.title: We assume life needs a sun, but a team at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich calculated in 2026 that moons orbiting starless rogue planets could sustain liquid oceans for up to 4.3 billion years — roughly as long as Earth has existed — without a single ra - Space Daily
item.description: We assume life needs a sun, but a team at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich calculated in 2026 that moons orbiting starless rogue planets could sustain liquid oceans for up to 4.3 billion years — roughly as long as Earth has existed — without a single ra  Space Daily

item.title: In 1970, Venera 7 appeared to fall silent when it hit Venus, but engineers later found 23 minutes of faint radio data buried in the recording — the first transmission ever received from the surface of another planet - Space Daily
item.description: In 1970, Venera 7 appeared to fall silent when it hit Venus, but engineers later found 23 minutes of faint radio data buried in the recording — the first transmission ever received from the surface of another planet  Space Daily

item.title: When Spirit’s front wheel failed on Mars, engineers drove the rover backwards and let the dead wheel scrape through the soil — accidentally uncovering bright silica that pointed to ancient hot springs or steam vents - Space Daily
item.description: When Spirit’s front wheel failed on Mars, engineers drove the rover backwards and let the dead wheel scrape through the soil — accidentally uncovering bright silica that pointed to ancient hot springs or steam vents  Space Daily

item.title: Cassini watched bright “islands” appear, disappear and reappear in Titan’s methane seas — and laboratory experiments suggest they may be fields of nitrogen bubbles fizzing out of solution as the alien lakes warm or mix - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Cassini watched bright “islands” appear, disappear and reappear in Titan’s methane seas — and laboratory experiments suggest they may be fields of nitrogen bubbles fizzing out of solution as the alien lakes warm or mix  Space Daily
  2. Saturn's moon Titan runs the same weather cycle as Earth — clouds, rain, rivers and seas — except the liquid doing all the work is methane, and the bedrock underfoot is water frozen at nearly minus 180 degrees, harder than most stone on Earth  Space Daily
  3. Saturn’s Largest Moon May Hold the Resources for a Space Colony  SciTechDaily
  4. Even at noon Titan sits in permanent dusk: distance and thick haze together leave barely a thousandth of Earth's sunlight at the surface — about as dim as Earth ten minutes after sunset, a golden gloom that never lifts  Space Daily
  5. Titan is the only place beyond Earth where a spacecraft has landed on a surface with liquid nearby, and the probe sent back the sound of alien wind before falling silent forever  Space Daily

item.title: Is Working Memory the Subconscious Engine of Consciousness? - Neuroscience News
item.description: Is Working Memory the Subconscious Engine of Consciousness?  Neuroscience News

item.title: Across Titan’s equator, winds have built dunes around 100 metres high and hundreds of kilometres long — but the grains are water ice coated in hydrocarbons that fell out of the moon’s orange atmosphere - Space Daily
item.description: Across Titan’s equator, winds have built dunes around 100 metres high and hundreds of kilometres long — but the grains are water ice coated in hydrocarbons that fell out of the moon’s orange atmosphere  Space Daily

item.title: Astronauts who have seen the whole Earth at once describe the overview effect — every war and border and argument hanging in silence — and afterwards traffic and small talk can feel strangely far away, like the world seen through glass - Space Daily
item.description: Astronauts who have seen the whole Earth at once describe the overview effect — every war and border and argument hanging in silence — and afterwards traffic and small talk can feel strangely far away, like the world seen through glass  Space Daily
